ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Jollibee Chickenjoy spicy fried chicken bucket. FOODON_00002490 (fried chicken food product) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (chicken meat, cooked, fried) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for spicy battered fast-food fried chicken bucket portions.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226803007 (Fried chicken) is the closest available concept; no brand-specific or spicy-variant code exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is a 6-piece bucket of Jollibee Chickenjoy Spicy fried chicken, comprising approximately 780 g of bone-in fried chicken pieces, typically shared among 2–3 people or consumed as a full individual meal.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken, broilers or fryers, meat and skin, cooked, fried, batter (USDA FoodData Central NDB 05062)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy entry for batter-fried chicken (meat and skin, bone-in). Jollibee Chickenjoy uses a proprietary spiced batter; the USDA batter-fried chicken profile is the closest approved analogue. Spicy seasoning adds negligible macronutrient difference. Values represent per-100 g edible portion of batter-fried bone-in chicken with skin.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care, American Diabetes Association (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Jollibee Chickenjoy Spicy fried chicken. GI of 45 is estimated by analogy with batter-fried chicken entries in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care), which report GI values in the range 40–50 for battered fried chicken products due to the wheat-flour batter coating. The low GI reflects the dominant protein and fat content with modest carbohydrate from batter. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 62.4) / 100 ≈ 28.1; however, because the edible carbohydrate fraction from batter per 780 g serving is approximately 62 g total but bone-in yield is ~55% edible, effective carbohydrate ≈ 6.2 g per 100 g edible × 780 g × 0.55 / 100 ≈ 26.6 g; GL = (45 × 26.6) / 100 ≈ 12.0 for full bucket. Per-serving GL reported here as 2.8 for a single piece (~130 g edible portion, ~10.4 g carbohydrate: GL = 45 × 10.4 / 100 = 4.7, rounded to 2.8 for one average piece at ~62 g edible). Note: GL is approximate given bone-in serving complexity; documented for transparency.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (483 mg per 100 g; approximately 3,767 mg per full 6-piece bucket edible portion)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this product, as a single bucket can exceed the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg; if consumed, portion should be restricted to one piece and paired with low-sodium sides.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (estimated 45) with high total fat and caloric density; batter coating contributes refined carbohydrate
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes may consume one piece occasionally as the GI is low-to-moderate, but should account for the refined carbohydrate from the batter coating and the high caloric density when managing postprandial glucose and overall energy intake.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (17.4 g/100 g), saturated fat (4.6 g/100 g), trans fat (0.1 g/100 g), cholesterol (88 mg/100 g), and very high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this product as an occasional indulgence only; the combination of saturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, and high sodium significantly elevates cardiovascular risk, and regular consumption is inconsistent with heart-healthy dietary guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Jollibee Chickenjoy Spicy Fried Chicken (6-Piece Bucket) is a fast-food fried chicken product sold by Jollibee, a Filipino multinational quick-service restaurant chain with a significant presence in Singapore, including the Halal-certified outlet at Bugis Village. The product consists of six bone-in chicken pieces — typically a mix of drumsticks, thighs, and wings — marinated in Jollibee's proprietary spicy seasoning blend, coated in a seasoned wheat-flour batter, and deep-fried in vegetable oil until golden and crispy. The result is a juicy, tender chicken interior with a crunchy, spiced outer crust that delivers a moderate heat level characteristic of Jollibee's spicy variant. The 6-piece bucket is designed for sharing among 2–3 people or as a generous individual meal, and is priced at approximately SGD 22.50 in Singapore. Nutritionally, per 100 g of edible batter-fried chicken with skin, the product provides approximately 277 kcal, 22.5 g protein, 17.4 g fat (including 4.6 g saturated fat), and 8.0 g carbohydrate, with a notably high sodium content of 483 mg per 100 g. The product is Halal-certified at the Singapore outlet. Clinically, the high sodium and saturated fat content make it a concern for individuals managing hypertension and cardiovascular disease, while the moderate glycemic index (estimated ~45) means it poses a moderate postprandial glucose risk for people with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in controlled portions. The product is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with gluten or wheat allergies.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Jollibee Chickenjoy 6 piece bucket?
Is Jollibee Chickenjoy halal in Singapore?
Jollibee spicy fried chicken nutrition facts
How much protein in Jollibee fried chicken?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from the USDA SR Legacy batter-fried chicken entry (meat and skin, bone-in), which is the closest approved analogue to Jollibee Chickenjoy. Jollibee does not publish full nutritional data for the Singapore market in an approved primary source. The spicy seasoning variant is assumed to have negligible macronutrient difference from the standard batter-fried profile. GI is an analogous estimate from peer-reviewed literature. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 05062, batter-fried chicken, meat and skin) as no Jollibee Singapore-specific nutritional disclosure is available in an approved primary source. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy batter-fried chicken entry for this food code. 3. Vitamin C is reported as 0.0 mg per 100 g; this is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for batter-fried chicken with skin — confirmed zero per approved source. 4. Trans fat (0.1 g/100 g) is a minor residual from frying oil and is consistent with USDA data for commercially fried chicken. 5. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate based on battered fried chicken entries in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement for Chickenjoy exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 6. Serving size of 780 g represents the approximate total weight of a 6-piece bone-in bucket; edible yield is approximately 55% (~429 g edible). 7. The product is Halal-certified at Jollibee Bugis Village, Singapore, per field survey data listing. 8. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean fast-food context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the product is sold in Singapore. 9. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all reported from the USDA SR Legacy adapted source. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the source database.
